result.learned: Weco is a hosted "autoresearch engine": the open-source CLI (weco-cli, Apache-2.0) is a thin execution client for a server-side AIDE tree search. The contract of `weco run`: you give `--source`/`--sources` (max 10 files, 200KB each, 500KB total), an `--eval-command` (arbitrary shell), a single `--metric` name + `--goal max|min`, and `--steps` (default 100). The backend LLM proposes candidate code, the CLI swaps candidates into the local files, runs the eval command as a LOCAL subprocess, and posts the raw terminal output back; the backend parses the metric SERVER-SIDE and generates the next candidate (weco/optimizer.py: get/claim execution tasks → run_evaluation_with_files_swap → submit_execution_result). Source code and eval output are therefore uploaded to Weco's cloud. Auth is an OAuth 2.0 device flow (`weco login`: POST {base}/auth/device/initiate → browser → poll /auth/device/token); the token lands in ~/.config/weco/credentials.json (0600), overridable by WECO_API_KEY — so "hook your Weco login" concretely means: the user runs `weco login` once locally and every subsequent CLI call authenticates from that file; agentdex never needs to touch it. Billing is Weco-side credits (`weco credits balance/topup/autotopup`, per-step cost shown in dashboard); BYO LLM keys are supported via `--api-key gemini=K openai=K anthropic=K` (defaults gemini-3-flash-preview / o4-mini / claude-opus-4-5), but whether BYOK zeroes credit consumption is unverified (FAQ answers are client-side-rendered accordions). Crucially, Weco has NO agent-spawn capability — it cannot literally "start Claude Code." The documented direction is inverted: `weco setup claude-code` installs a skill so Claude Code drives weco (background `weco run --output plain`, poll `weco run status`, steer with `weco run derive` which stops the parent and inherits lineage-best as step 0, `instruct/stop/resume`, results via `weco run results --format json|csv`, `overview` for the whole lineage tree). The GOALS.md phrase is nonetheless implementable three sanctioned ways: (b) weco-as-mutator-of-the-agent-genome — `--sources` = the agent's prompt/skill/config files, `--eval-command` = a ladder adapter that launches headless Claude Code with those files against benchmark tasks and prints the score, so weco's tree search literally drives Claude Code executions; (c) review mode — `weco run --require-review` + `review/revise/submit` ("This is how agents interact with review-mode runs"), letting Claude Code inspect or REPLACE each candidate before local eval, making it a co-mutation operator inside weco's search; (d) `weco observe init/log` (+ Python SDK WecoObserver, branching via --parent-step, idempotent steps, multi-metric JSON with one primary) — agentdex/bene owns the whole loop and weco is only tracking/dashboard. aideml is the open-source ancestor: agentic tree search where search_policy drafts until num_drafts exist, debugs buggy leaves with probability debug_prob (bounded by max_debug_depth), else greedily improves the best node; feedback via an LLM function-call (review_func_spec) parsing exec results; execution via a persistent Python child process using exec() with SIGINT→SIGKILL timeout — Python-only, so for driving arbitrary agents the weco-cli eval-command contract is strictly superior. AIDE paper: arXiv 2502.13138 (2025-02-18, Jiang et al.), SOTA on MLE-Bench/RE-Bench; README claims 4× more MLE-bench medals than the best linear agent (OpenHands). Biggest architectural fact for agentdex: weco optimizes ONE scalar metric per run — Pareto frontier measurement cannot be delegated to it; weco is the scalarized inner loop, and agentdex's Pareto engine + bene meta-harness must sit outside, choosing per-run scalarizations/directions (via `derive -i`) and reconstructing the frontier from per-step trajectories (`results --format json`, `overview` global_step ordering). Langfuse/langsmith integrations replace the shell eval with dataset+evaluator backends (`--eval-backend langfuse`, target `module:function`, metric function combines evaluator scores into a single number).

evidence.note: weco run flags; in-place file modification warning; eval contract: run modified code, print '<metric>: <value>' to terminal (format flexible), non-zero exit or poor score to signal buggy solutions; reward-hacking guidance; AIDE algorithm named as the engine

observe init/log CLI + Python SDK (weco.observe.WecoObserver), step-0 baseline, --parent-step branching, idempotent steps, 24h auto-archive; run-vs-observe split table; dashboard: solution tree, credits/billing, auto top-up, remote stop; 'sign in with the same Google account used in the CLI'

full command surface: run status/overview/results/show/diff/derive/stop/instruct/review/revise/submit, resume, login/logout, credits; --steps default 100, --log-dir .runs, --output plain, --require-review; --api-key provider=key with per-provider defaults; derive semantics (stops parent, inherits lineage-best as step 0); review-mode workflow quoted as 'how agents interact with review-mode runs'; skill has Claude Code run weco in background and even run `weco login` itself

loop mechanics: CLI polls/claims execution tasks, runs eval locally via run_evaluation_with_files_swap, submit_execution_result posts terminal output; backend does LLM candidate generation and metric parsing server-side; source_code dict uploaded at start_run; /runs/{id}/share endpoint for public share links

OAuth device flow (auth/device/initiate + auth/device/token, device_code grant); API key at ~/.config/weco/credentials.json chmod 600; WECO_API_KEY env takes precedence; DEFAULT_MODELS = [(gemini, gemini-3-flash-preview), (openai, o4-mini), (anthropic, claude-opus-4-5)]; installation.json anonymous telemetry ID

AIDE = agentic tree search in code space; search_policy: draft until num_drafts, debug buggy leaves w/ debug_prob bounded by max_debug_depth, else greedy improve of best node; review_func_spec LLM feedback; persistent Python-only exec() interpreter w/ SIGINT→SIGKILL timeout; config agent.code.model/agent.steps/agent.search.num_drafts; MLE-bench '4x more medals than best linear agent (OpenHands)'; paper Jiang et al., 2025-02-18; no external-agent plug-in point

weco-cli Apache-2.0, ~57 stars; providers OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Concentrate AI/Kimi, default gemini-3-flash-preview; FAQ page confirms question headings (Do I need my own LLM API keys? / How much do optimizations cost? / What data does Weco send to the cloud? / Can I run multiple runs in parallel?) but answers are client-side accordions — not extractable, left open

--eval-backend langfuse, --langfuse-dataset, --langfuse-target module:function; code evaluators run locally + managed LLM-as-judge evaluators; metric function must combine evaluator scores into a single number (reinforces single-scalar contract)

next: Synthesis lane: pick the weco-hook mechanism for ADR-0015 — recommended hybrid: pattern (a) Claude Code drives weco via the official skill (documented, zero custom glue) layered on pattern (b) agent-genome-as-sources with a ladder-adapter eval command that launches headless Claude Code; keep (d) weco-observe as the lock-in-free fallback where bene mh owns the loop

Empirically verify pricing/limits that the FAQ hides: run `weco login` + `weco credits balance` on a fresh account (free-credit amount), one 2-step toy run (credit burn per step), and test whether --api-key BYOK reduces credit consumption

Prototype the E2E slice: --sources = one skill/prompt file, --eval-command = script running `claude -p` against 3 toy benchmark tasks printing 'score: N', --steps 3 --output plain --eval-timeout generous; measure wall-clock, cost, and whether trajectories from `weco run results --format json` suffice for Pareto ledger ingestion

Define agentdex's Pareto measurement contract as OWNED by agentdex (weco is single-scalar): per-objective scalarization runs or derive-branches, frontier reconstruction from per-step JSON trajectories + local re-measurement of secondary axes
Check Weco ToS/privacy before designing the website integration (source code + eval output are uploaded to Weco's backend; share-link embedding of runs into the agentdex site needs ToS review)
key_facts: `weco run` contract: --source|--sources (≤10 files, 200KB each, 500KB total) + --eval-command (any shell) + one --metric + --goal max|min + --steps (default 100); eval script must execute the modified code and print '<metric>: <number>' to terminal (flexible format); source files are modified IN PLACE during the run

Split of labor: evaluation runs LOCALLY as a subprocess on the user's machine; candidate generation AND metric parsing run SERVER-SIDE on Weco's hosted backend — the CLI uploads source code at run start and raw eval terminal output every step (weco/optimizer.py, weco/api.py)

Auth = OAuth 2.0 device flow via `weco login`; token stored at ~/.config/weco/credentials.json (0600); WECO_API_KEY env var takes precedence; dashboard uses the same account (Google sign-in per docs); `weco logout` clears it

Billing = Weco credits (balance/topup/autotopup; per-step cost in dashboard); BYO LLM API keys via `--api-key gemini=K openai=K anthropic=K`; DEFAULT_MODELS: gemini-3-flash-preview, o4-mini, claude-opus-4-5; weco-cli itself is Apache-2.0 open source

Weco CANNOT spawn agents — no subprocess/agent-command mutation support exists in weco-cli or aideml; the documented integration is the inverse: `weco setup claude-code` installs a skill so Claude Code drives weco (background run, `weco run status` polling, `derive` steering, `results --format json`)

Agent-in-the-loop IS supported via review mode: `weco run --require-review` → `weco run review` (pending nodes w/ plan+code) → `weco run revise/submit --node <id> [--source <file>]` — an external agent can replace Weco's proposed code before local evaluation ('This is how agents interact with review-mode runs')

`weco run derive <run-id> --from-step best -i "..."` stops the parent, inherits the lineage-best solution as step 0 without re-evaluation, and starts a fresh-context branch; `weco run overview` returns the whole lineage tree with global_step ordering — a natural fit for evolution-card lineages

weco optimizes exactly ONE scalar metric per run (single --metric/--goal; langfuse/langsmith backends likewise require a metric function combining evaluator scores into a single number); multi-metric exists only in `weco observe log --metrics '{...}'` with one primary — Pareto is out of weco's scope

`weco observe init/log` (+ Python SDK WecoObserver) tracks any EXTERNAL optimization loop: step-0 baseline, idempotent step re-posting, --parent-step tree branching, runs auto-archive after 24h idle — the zero-lock-in path where bene/agentdex owns the loop and weco is dashboard only

aideml (open source, AIDE algorithm, arXiv 2502.13138, Jiang et al. 2025) = tree search: draft until agent.search.num_drafts, debug buggy leaves with debug_prob (≤ max_debug_depth), else greedily improve best node; LLM-function-call feedback (review_func_spec); persistent Python-only exec() interpreter — self-hostable fallback but Python-only, unlike weco-cli's language-agnostic eval-command
AIDE performance claim: SOTA on Kaggle evals, OpenAI MLE-Bench, METR RE-Bench; '4× more medals than the best linear agent (OpenHands)' on MLE-bench (README)
design_implications: The GOALS.md hook 'Weco starts Claude Code' must be re-architected as one of two concrete mechanisms (weco cannot spawn agents): (A) agentdex CLI launches Claude Code with the official weco skill and a task brief — Claude Code is the orchestrator that runs/monitors/steers `weco run` (documented, supported, zero custom glue); or (B) weco-as-genome-mutator — `weco run --sources <agent prompt/skill/config files>` where the --eval-command is an agentdex ladder adapter that launches headless Claude Code (`claude -p`) with the mutated genome against benchmark tasks and prints the score; here Weco's hosted AIDE search literally drives Claude Code executions each step. Recommended: A+B combined (Claude Code supervises a type-B run)

BYO creds maps cleanly: user's Claude Code subscription powers agent execution locally (inside the eval subprocess); user's `weco login` (~/.config/weco/credentials.json or WECO_API_KEY) powers the mutation search; optional --api-key offsets LLM cost. agentdex never holds either credential — it only needs a preflight (`weco credits balance` succeeds) exactly as the official skill does. Note: a Claude Code subscription is NOT an Anthropic API key, so it cannot serve as weco's --api-key anthropic=… — weco's mutation LLM is paid via weco credits or a real API key

Weco is a single-scalar inner loop — the Pareto engine CANNOT be outsourced to it. Architecture: agentdex Pareto engine + bene meta-harness = outer loop choosing task type, scalarization/direction, and budget; weco run/derive = inner loop per direction; frontier reconstructed from `weco run results --format json` + `weco run overview` (per-step metric trajectories, lineage tree, global_step) plus local re-measurement of secondary axes (cost/latency) that weco doesn't track

Genome constraint shapes the agent representation: whatever agentdex calls 'the self-evolving agent' must distill to ≤10 mutable text files, ≤200KB each, ≤500KB total (CLAUDE.md/skills/config), with everything else frozen in the eval harness — this should be a first-class schema in the design doc

Privacy is a product-surface fact: weco uploads the genome source and all eval terminal output to its backend. The agentdex ladder adapters must scrub benchmark data/PII from eval stdout, and the website must disclose this; for privacy-hard users, ship the weco-observe fallback (bene mh owns mutations locally, weco only gets logged metrics+code the user opts into) or self-hosted aideml

weco's lineage/derive model is near-isomorphic to ADR-0010's Evolution Card: run = evolution episode, derive = branch from lineage-best, overview tree = card lineage view; the agentdex ledger should store weco run_id/lineage_id and can embed dashboard share links (/runs/{id}/share) in the website — pending ToS check

Budget guardrails are mandatory: --steps defaults to 100 and agent-benchmark evals are slow/expensive — agentdex briefs must always pin small explicit --steps, --eval-timeout, and use `weco run status` polling + `stop` (skill already encodes this discipline); credits pricing is opaque (open question) so the loop needs a user-visible cost ceiling

Review mode (--require-review + submit --node --source) is the highest-leverage differentiator for 'meta-harness + Weco': bene mh can act as a candidate FILTER/REWRITER between Weco's proposal and local evaluation — outer-loop skill/memory (mh_search, skill_apply) injected per-node without forking weco
open_questions: Exact credit pricing (cost per step/candidate) and free-credit amount at signup — FAQ answers are client-side accordions; only second-hand search synthesis says 'fractions of a cent per candidate'. Verify empirically with a fresh account
Does BYO --api-key reduce or eliminate credit consumption for `weco run`, or are credits charged regardless (backend still runs the search)?
Weco Observe free tier '10k experiments/month' — appeared only in search-engine synthesis attributed to weco.ai; no primary page found (weco.ai/observe 404s). Unverified

Official privacy answer to 'What data does Weco send to the cloud?' (heading exists in FAQ; answer unread — code inspection shows source files + eval output uploaded, but retention/training-use policy unknown)
Does Weco expose a public server-to-server API (read a user's runs with their token) that the agentdex website could use, beyond per-run public share links? Nothing documented

Parallel runs: FAQ heading 'Can I run multiple Weco runs in parallel?' exists but the answer is unread; weco/consumer_lock.py + the skill's 'first run becomes the lineage's evaluation consumer' suggest one eval consumer per lineage — matters for parallel ladder measurement

Minor doc discrepancy: docs-seed cli-reference shows a --model flag on `weco run`, while the newer skill reference shows --api-key with per-provider defaults and supported-models says default gemini-3-flash-preview — current flag surface should be confirmed against `weco run --help` of the installed version
Whether Weco's ToS permits embedding/mirroring dashboard data (share links, solution trees) in a third-party market site like agentdex
